rationale: In the theory section, the citation is used at a specific conceptual point rather than as a generic background reference: the authors explicitly invoke Van Heerde et al. (2000) terminology to frame the expected temporal sales pattern. They use it to justify an anticipated immediate sales dip after assortment reduction (from postponement) and later partial recovery. The citing paper does not adopt the cited paper's full estimation model, so the role is conceptual transfer rather than direct methodological extension.
relation_description: The citing paper borrows Van Heerde et al. (2000)'s dip-dynamics concept and terminology, labeling the immediate decline after delisting as a "post-assortment reduction dip." It applies this promotion-based dynamic logic to assortment reduction to motivate short-term versus long-term effect expectations.
